name: Franky Barragan
title: Technical Account Management • Community Management • IT Professional — Open to Opportunities
bio: I'm an infrastructure engineer and technical community leader with over a decade of experience architecting resilient virtual infrastructure and leading global technical communities. I've managed 1,000+ VMs across on-premises and hybrid cloud environments, and led a 23,000+ member developer community through certification programs, hackathons, and a major corporate transition. I bring the same rigor to systems and to people — building scalable, data-driven programs that turn technical depth into real community and business impact.

certifications.name: VMware Certified Professional – Data Center Virtualization (VCP-DCV)
VMware Cloud on AWS – Trained Professional
Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion in the Workplace Certificate

story_title: Bridging Deep Technical Work and Community Impact
story_text: My career has run on two parallel tracks that turned out to reinforce each other: hands-on infrastructure engineering, and community leadership at scale. On the technical side, I spent eight years as the primary engineer responsible for a 1,000+ VM environment, turning weekly outages into a rare event through root cause analysis and proactive hardening. On the community side, I've led global programs for tens of thousands of members, mentored dozens of volunteer leaders, and kept a community's trust intact through one of its most disruptive transitions. I've found the best community programs are built with the same discipline as good infrastructure: understand the real failure points, build for resilience, and measure what actually matters.
anecdote_title: Turning a Weekly Fire Drill Into a Non-Event
anecdote_text: For four years, I was the only VMware engineer responsible for infrastructure supporting over 1,000 virtual machines — and P1 incidents were hitting us almost every week. Rather than keep firefighting, I dug into the root cause of every recurring failure pattern, hardened the environment against what kept resurfacing, and built out proactive monitoring so problems surfaced before they became outages. Within a few years, P1 incidents dropped from weekly to fewer than one a year — and stayed there for the rest of my time at the company.

soft_skills.anecdote: Served as the sole on-call VMware engineer for four years, then remained the primary escalation point for eight, keeping mission-critical infrastructure stable without a dedicated backup.
Launched a VMware Cloud Foundation exam prep series that hit 1,000+ signups in 48 hours, adapting format and schedule in real time based on community feedback.
Mentored 60+ volunteer VMUG leaders across the western US, building reusable playbooks that let new leaders launch chapters faster.
